name: email_change_confirm

metadata.description: Email change confirmation link sent to the NEW address (sent by _send_email_change_confirm during on_email_change_request).
notes: `user` is a dict (user.to_dict('basic')) representing the current account holder.
`token` is an ec: token generated server-side via tokens.generate_email_change_token(user, new_email).
`new_email` is the new email address the user wants to change to.
This email is sent to new_email (not user.email) — the recipient address will differ from the account on file.
Token has a 1-hour TTL and is single-use. Redeeming it commits the new email and rotates all active sessions.
This framework does not define the frontend confirmation URL. Include a placeholder link pattern that projects can customize.

text_template:
Hello{% if user.display_name %} {{ user.display_name }}{% endif %},

A request was made to change your account email address to:
{{ new_email }}

Use the token below to confirm this change.

Confirmation token:
{{ token }}

If your project supports confirmation links, your URL typically looks like:
https://YOUR_APP_HOST/confirm-email-change?token={{ token }}

This link expires in 1 hour.

Once confirmed, all other active sessions will be signed out for your security.

If you did not request this change, you can ignore this email. Your current email address will remain unchanged.
html_template:
Confirm your new email address

Hello{% if user.display_name %} {{ user.display_name }}{% endif %},

A request was made to change your account email address to {{ new_email }}. Use the token below to confirm this change.

Confirmation token
{{ token }}

If your project supports confirmation links, your URL typically looks like:
https://YOUR_APP_HOST/confirm-email-change?token={{ token }}

This link expires in 1 hour. Once confirmed, all other active sessions will be signed out for your security.

If you did not request this change, you can ignore this email. Your current email address will remain unchanged.

Sent by Django-MOJO
